guide.caption: LuckyJ 9s, NAGA 3s
guide.situation: South 3-5, middle hand, 38 tiles left. LuckyJ is lead on 54100 points in rank 1. After drawing [[3s]], LuckyJ cuts [[9s]]; NAGA's top line is [[3s]]. Result: kamicha won, self delta -4500

guide.limit: Copy the surface tile only when the score job, threat level, and next discard chain match.
guide.prompt: With 38 tiles left, would you cut [[9s]] or [[3s]]? First name what [[3s]] is preserving.
guide.answer: LuckyJ chooses [[9s]]. The reproducible reason is: The lead seat changes the price of this discard. LuckyJ chooses the discard that leaves the next turn manageable.

guide_ja.caption: LuckyJ 9s、NAGA 3s

guide.caption: pon on 5sr, then 6s
guide.situation: South 4-0, late hand, 1 tiles left. LuckyJ is danger zone on 0 points in rank 4. LuckyJ calls pon on [[5sr]] from the left player, then cuts [[6s]]. Result: draw, self delta -1500
guide.read: The closed route is running out of practical turns, so the pon turns a poor closed shape into a real hand.
guide.whyNot: Passing is tempting because it keeps the hand closed. With 1 tiles left, the closed ideal may run out of useful turns.
guide.copy: Copy the pon only when it creates yaku, speed, tenpai pressure, or denial, and when the first post-call discard [[6s]] is already planned.
guide.limit: The call needs a named post-call discard and a next defensive exit to become LuckyJ-style tempo.
guide.prompt: Would you pon on [[5sr]]? Before answering, name the discard after the call.
guide.answer: Here the call is coherent because [[6s]] is already the release tile. The closed route is running out of practical turns, so the pon turns a poor closed shape into a real hand.

guide_ja.caption: pon して 6s
guide_ja.situation: South 4-0、late、残り1枚。danger zone、0点、着順4。LuckyJ は 下家 から [[5sr]] を pon し、[[6s]] を切る。結果: draw, self delta -1500
guide_ja.read: 閉じた手が間に合いにくいので、pon で実戦的な手に変えている。
guide_ja.whyNot: 門前維持は自然な選択。ただしこの局面では残り枚数と手牌 5s 5s から、門前の理想形を待つ余裕が薄い。
guide_ja.copy: pon が役、速度、テンパイ、または相手への圧力を作る時だけ真似する。鳴いた後の最初の打牌 [[6s]] まで先に決める。
guide_ja.limit: 鳴いた後の安全牌や次の方針まで言える時に、LuckyJ 型のテンポになる。
guide_ja.prompt: [[5sr]] を pon するか。答える前に、鳴いた後に何を切るかを言う。
guide_ja.answer: この例では [[6s]] まで見えているため、閉じた手が間に合いにくいので、pon で実戦的な手に変えている。

guide.caption: LuckyJ 4s, NAGA 9s
guide.situation: East 4-3, middle hand, 29 tiles left. LuckyJ is lead on 35300 points in rank 3. After drawing [[4m]], LuckyJ cuts [[4s]]; NAGA's top line is [[9s]]. Result: toimen won, self delta -6300

point-06.title: Build the value early
point-06.lesson: When the score job needs more, optimizing toward a cheap bad-shape future is too small. Early acceptance is the cheapest thing to spend.
point-06.prompt: Ask how many points this hand needs before preserving maximum ukeire.
point-06.answer: Keep dora, yaku, and shape upgrades when the fast hand would be too small or too brittle.

guide.caption: pon on W, then C
guide.situation: East 3-0, late hand, 24 tiles left. LuckyJ is above starting score on 27300 points in rank 2. LuckyJ calls pon on [[W]] from the right player, then cuts [[C]]. Result: toimen won, self delta -700
guide.read: The pon buys tempo with 24 tiles left. LuckyJ is changing the round clock and completing a useful set.
guide.whyNot: Passing is tempting because it keeps the hand closed. With 24 tiles left, the closed ideal may run out of useful turns.
guide.copy: Copy the pon only when it creates yaku, speed, tenpai pressure, or denial, and when the first post-call discard [[C]] is already planned.
guide.limit: The call needs a named post-call discard and a next defensive exit to become LuckyJ-style tempo.
guide.prompt: Would you pon on [[W]]? Before answering, name the discard after the call.
guide.answer: Here the call is coherent because [[C]] is already the release tile. The pon buys tempo with 24 tiles left. LuckyJ is changing the round clock and completing a useful set.

guide_ja.caption: pon して C
guide_ja.situation: East 3-0、late、残り24枚。above starting score、27300点、着順2。LuckyJ は 上家 から [[W]] を pon し、[[C]] を切る。結果: toimen won, self delta -700
guide_ja.read: pon は速度を買うための副露。残り24枚で、閉じたまま待つより局面の時計を進めている。
guide_ja.whyNot: 門前維持は自然な選択。ただしこの局面では残り枚数と手牌 W W から、門前の理想形を待つ余裕が薄い。
guide_ja.copy: pon が役、速度、テンパイ、または相手への圧力を作る時だけ真似する。鳴いた後の最初の打牌 [[C]] まで先に決める。
guide_ja.limit: 鳴いた後の安全牌や次の方針まで言える時に、LuckyJ 型のテンポになる。
guide_ja.prompt: [[W]] を pon するか。答える前に、鳴いた後に何を切るかを言う。
guide_ja.answer: この例では [[C]] まで見えているため、pon は速度を買うための副露。残り24枚で、閉じたまま待つより局面の時計を進めている。

guide.situation: East 4-1, late hand, 22 tiles left. LuckyJ is above starting score on 28000 points in rank 1. LuckyJ calls pon on [[5s]] from the across player, then cuts [[7s]]. Result: self won 4 han 30 fu, delta +8000
guide.read: The pon buys tempo with 22 tiles left. LuckyJ is changing the round clock and completing a useful set.
guide.whyNot: Passing is tempting because it keeps the hand closed. With 22 tiles left, the closed ideal may run out of useful turns.
guide.copy: Copy the pon only when it creates yaku, speed, tenpai pressure, or denial, and when the first post-call discard [[7s]] is already planned.
guide.limit: The call needs a named post-call discard and a next defensive exit to become LuckyJ-style tempo.
guide.prompt: Would you pon on [[5s]]? Before answering, name the discard after the call.
guide.answer: Here the call is coherent because [[7s]] is already the release tile. The pon buys tempo with 22 tiles left. LuckyJ is changing the round clock and completing a useful set.

guide_ja.caption: pon して 7s
guide_ja.situation: East 4-1、late、残り22枚。above starting score、28000点、着順1。LuckyJ は 対面 から [[5s]] を pon し、[[7s]] を切る。結果: self won 4 han 30 fu, delta +8000

guide_ja.whyNot: 門前維持は自然な選択。ただしこの局面では残り枚数と手牌 5s 5s から、門前の理想形を待つ余裕が薄い。

guide_ja.limit: 鳴いた後の安全牌や次の方針まで言える時に、LuckyJ 型のテンポになる。
guide_ja.prompt: [[5s]] を pon するか。答える前に、鳴いた後に何を切るかを言う。
guide_ja.answer: この例では [[7s]] まで見えているため、pon は速度を買うための副露。残り22枚で、閉じたまま待つより局面の時計を進めている。

point-07.title: Call for tempo with a purpose
point-07.lesson: The call has a job beyond completing blocks: speed up a poor hand, contest the table, break a bonus window, or preserve draw equity.
point-07.prompt: Name the purpose before exposing the hand.
point-07.answer: A good tempo call changes the round's clock and keeps you prepared for the next threat.

point-08.title: Riichi converts pressure into equity
point-08.lesson: LuckyJ can declare before the shape is perfect when riichi itself changes the table. The declaration can force rivals to fold, deny calls, or lock in placement value.
point-08.prompt: Before riichi, ask who must now respond to you and whether better upgrades are realistic.
point-08.answer: If the wait is serviceable and the table must respect the stick, declaration pressure can be worth more than waiting.

guide.caption: LuckyJ 5p, NAGA 7p
guide.situation: South 1-1, middle hand, 39 tiles left. LuckyJ is danger zone on 14400 points in rank 2. After drawing [[C]], LuckyJ cuts [[5p]]; NAGA's top line is [[7p]]. Result: self won 3 han 50 fu, delta +10700
guide.read: Riichi is part of the value calculation. With a declaration probability of 99.7%, LuckyJ treats pressure as a real output of the discard.
guide.whyNot: The NAGA line through [[7p]] is tempting because it leaves 0-shanten, 6 visible ukeire, main accepts [[3p]], [[6p]]. LuckyJ's [[5p]] line leaves 0-shanten, 3 visible ukeire, main accepts [[5m]], [[7p]]. The danger numbers are close: LuckyJ 88.6%, NAGA 88.6%.
guide.copy: Copy the pressure only when riichi changes opponent behavior and the wait is good enough to make that pressure matter.
guide.limit: Declare with a reason. Bad waits, huge placement punishment, or a clear upgrade can still make damaten correct.
guide.prompt: With 39 tiles left, would you cut [[5p]] or [[7p]]? First name what [[7p]] is preserving.
guide.answer: LuckyJ chooses [[5p]]. The reproducible reason is: Riichi is part of the value calculation. With a declaration probability of 99.7%, LuckyJ treats pressure as a real output of the discard.

guide_ja.caption: LuckyJ 5p、NAGA 7p
guide_ja.situation: South 1-1、middle、残り39枚。danger zone、14400点、着順2。LuckyJ は [[C]] ツモから [[5p]] を切り、NAGA 第一候補は [[7p]]。結果: self won 3 han 50 fu, delta +10700
guide_ja.read: リーチ自体が価値になる局面。宣言確率 99.7% を含めて、圧力を打牌の成果として見ている。
guide_ja.whyNot: NAGA の [[7p]] は 0シャンテン、見えている受け入れ6枚、主な受け [[3p]], [[6p]] を残すので魅力がある。LuckyJ の [[5p]] は 0シャンテン、見えている受け入れ3枚、主な受け [[5m]], [[7p]]。危険度は近い。LuckyJ 88.6%、NAGA 88.6%。
guide_ja.copy: リーチで相手の行動が変わり、待ちも最低限ある時だけ圧力を真似する。
guide_ja.limit: リーチには理由を持たせる。悪形、着順罰、明確な改良があればダマも残る。
guide_ja.prompt: 残り39枚で [[5p]] と [[7p]] のどちらを切るか。先に [[7p]] が何を残しているかを言う。
guide_ja.answer: LuckyJ は [[5p]] を選ぶ。理由は リーチ自体が価値になる局面。宣言確率 99.7% を含めて、圧力を打牌の成果として見ている。

point-13.answer: When your hand is too slow to punish them and the yakuhai has no strong job, clean it before the open hand gets to use it.

point-16.title: Late outside cuts can preserve the route
point-16.lesson: Under pressure, cutting an edge or terminal can be the attacking discard because it keeps the middle tile that connects the actual path to tenpai.

point-16.answer: Treat the outside cut as route preservation when the outside tile carries little value or target-specific safety.
